topic: ChatGPT prompting strategies and real-world use cases

entries.a: Prompts.chat is the world's largest open-source prompt library for AI, designed to work with various AI models like ChatGPT, Claude, and others.

entries.q: What types of prompts can be found on prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat offers a curated collection of prompt examples for AI chat models, suitable for both beginners and advanced users.

entries.q: How can I contribute to prompts.chat?
entries.a: Users can contribute prompts by visiting prompts.chat/prompts/new, where their submissions will sync automatically to the library.

entries.q: What is the Interactive Book of Prompting?
entries.a: The Interactive Book of Prompting is a free, interactive guide that teaches prompt engineering through 25+ chapters, covering topics from basics to advanced techniques.

entries.q: What is the target age group for the 'Prompting for Kids' program?
entries.a: The 'Prompting for Kids' program is designed for children aged 8-14, using interactive games to teach them how to communicate with AI.

entries.q: What are the self-hosting options available for prompts.chat?
entries.a: Users can deploy their own private prompt library with custom branding and authentication using a quick start command or manual setup instructions provided in the documentation.

entries.q: What integrations are available for prompts.chat?
entries.a: Prompts.chat can be integrated with various tools, including a command-line interface (CLI) and as an MCP server for AI tools.

entries.q: How has prompts.chat been recognized in the academic community?
entries.a: Prompts.chat has been referenced by institutions like Harvard and Columbia and has received over 40 academic citations, highlighting its significance in effective AI use.
